assumptions: q and p are normalised probability densities on the same support
absolute continuity holds before evaluating D_KL
likelihood values are strictly positive or explicitly regularised
complexity and accuracy terms are reported separately before interpretation
executable_validation_targets: verify KL complexity term is non-negative on shared support
verify complexity plus accuracy decomposition equals total F
verify free-energy upper-bound relation against negative log evidence
null_controls: support-mismatch control must reject D_KL evaluation
identical-density control must produce zero KL complexity
zero-likelihood control must reject accuracy evaluation
specs.section_path: Structural Overview (by Domain) > Computational Unifier. > I. The Free Energy Principle: The Imperative to Minimise Surprise
validation_targets: complexity plus accuracy decomposition equals total F
surprise upper-bound check against negative log evidence

variables.p_theta_y: posterior density over causes after observing sensory data y
variables.p_y_theta: generative likelihood of sensory data under causes theta
variables.q_theta_mu: recognition density over causes under internal state mu

summary.policy: EQ0130-EQ0131 anchors are promoted only into validation specifications. Free-energy and predictive-coding interpretations remain finite mathematical-boundary checks until executable falsification controls pass.
